task_yaml.task:
I'm putting together a sync-licensing pitch and I need a proper
breakdown of a track before I send it out. The file is at
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/song.wav. Use the music-analysis skill to
analyze it — I need the tempo in BPM, the musical key, section
structure with timestamps, and a one-paragraph summary of how the
energy flows through the track.

Save the human-readable write-up to /tmp_workspace/results/analysis.md
and a structured sidecar to /tmp_workspace/results/sections.json so I
can grep it programmatically.

A few specifics:
- Tempo as BPM rounded to one decimal place.
- Key as tonic plus mode, e.g. C major or A minor.
- For sections, list every section in source order with a start time,
  end time, and a label drawn from the usual pop vocabulary — intro,
  verse, pre-chorus, chorus, bridge, outro. Don't merge them into
  one block, and don't invent extra ones.
- In the prose summary tell me how the energy moves from start to
  end and which section is the loudest moment of the track.
- The sidecar JSON should at minimum carry bpm, key, and a sections
  array where each entry has start, end, and label.
